evomonCodes.title: Evomon Codes and Redeem Guide
evomonCodes.subtitle: Use active Evomon codes to claim launch rewards such as Coins, Medium EXP Fruits, and milestone gifts.
evomonCodes.intro: Evomon codes are limited-time reward strings released around launch, like goals, and community events. Copy each code exactly, finish the opening tutorial when required, and redeem from the cogwheel Settings menu before the code expires.

codes.bestUse: Redeem after opening the Settings menu, then use the reward to support early progression.
Use Coins for early purchases and basic progression support.
Feed EXP Fruits to an Evomon you plan to keep in your first battle team.
Redeem early because it gives both leveling help and spendable currency.

redeemSteps: Launch Evomon from the official Roblox experience page.
Finish the opening tutorial if the Settings menu is not available yet.
Click the cogwheel Settings button near the top of the screen.
Paste one active code into the code input box.
Press OK to claim the reward.

troubleshooting.title: Why an Evomon Code May Not Work
checks: The code was typed with the wrong capitalization.
An extra space was pasted before or after the code.
The code was already claimed on the same Roblox account.
The code expired after a limited-time launch or milestone window.
The server is old and needs a fresh rejoin before new codes register.

evomonOfficialLinks.title: Evomon Official Roblox Link and Game Info
evomonOfficialLinks.subtitle: Play Evomon from the official Roblox page by Evomon Devs and use the linked official media channels for trailers, updates, and community notices.
evomonOfficialLinks.intro: Evomon is a Roblox monster-catching adventure built around catching, training, battling, exploring, egg hatching, rare Shiny and Sparkle forms, multiplayer play, and dungeon progression. Always start from the official Roblox experience instead of mirror pages.

evomonBeginnerGuide.title: Evomon Beginner Guide
evomonBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Follow a clean first-session route: join the official game, finish the tutorial, pick a starter, catch early Evomons, redeem codes, and build a useful first team.
evomonBeginnerGuide.intro: Evomon starts like a classic creature-collecting Roblox adventure: choose a starter, learn the battle and catching loop, then expand your team through wild encounters, rewards, eggs, rare variants, and exploration.

steps.title: Start from the Official Roblox Page
steps.description: Open Evomon[Release] by Evomon Devs and join from the official Roblox experience page.
steps.tip: Bookmark the official page so you do not land on copycat games or unrelated Evomon pages.

steps.title: Finish the Opening Tutorial
steps.description: Complete the first tutorial flow so the core menus, battle basics, catching flow, and code redemption area become easier to access.
steps.tip: Do not rush past menu explanations; the Settings menu is also where codes are redeemed.

steps.title: Choose a Starter
steps.description: Begin with one of the three starter choices: Leafbun, Blazpup, or Bubble.
steps.tip: Pick based on the first role you want in your party, then catch other types early to cover weaknesses.

steps.title: Enter the Early Exploration Loop
steps.description: Move into the opening areas, battle wild Evomons, catch new team members, and explore for treasures.
steps.tip: A wider early team is more useful than pouring every reward into only one creature.

steps.title: Redeem Launch Codes
steps.description: Open the cogwheel Settings menu, paste active codes, and claim Coins, EXP Fruits, or milestone rewards.
steps.tip: Use EXP Fruits on Evomons you are actually using in battles instead of scattering them across random catches.

steps.title: Build Element Coverage
steps.description: Add teammates that cover different battle situations instead of stacking several Evomons that do the same job.
steps.tip: A simple early team should include your starter, at least one different-element attacker, and a backup catch you can level safely.

steps.title: Train Before Chasing Rares
steps.description: Level your practical battle team before spending too much time chasing Shiny and Sparkle variants.
steps.tip: Rare forms are valuable collection goals, but early progress still depends on winning battles and unlocking more areas.

steps.title: Try Multiplayer and Dungeons
steps.description: Once your team has enough levels and coverage, team up with friends and begin using the dungeon side of the game loop.
steps.tip: Dungeons are easier when your party covers different roles instead of overlapping picks.

evomonBestStarter.title: Evomon Best Starter and Early Team Guide
evomonBestStarter.subtitle: Compare Leafbun, Blazpup, and Bubble by early role, coverage value, and first-team planning.
evomonBestStarter.intro: Evomon gives players three starter choices and then quickly pushes them into catching, training, battling, and team expansion. Use the starter as your first coverage slot, then catch different Evomons to balance the party.

starters.name: Leafbun
starters.progressionValue: Useful as a stable first-team member while you learn catching, training, and basic battle pacing.
starters.teamPlan: Pair Leafbun with Fire and Water coverage so the party does not depend on one matchup pattern.
starters.bestFor: Players who want a balanced first route and simple early team planning.
starters.avoid: Do not keep only Grass-style coverage in the party just because the starter feels comfortable.

starters.name: Blazpup
starters.progressionValue: Fits players who want a faster early battle rhythm and a clear attacking role in the early team.
starters.teamPlan: Add Water or Grass coverage early, then keep a backup catch leveled so difficult matchups do not stall progress.
starters.bestFor: Players who prefer faster early fights and attacker-focused progression.
starters.avoid: Do not spend every EXP Fruit on Blazpup before catching teammates with different coverage.

starters.name: Bubble
starters.progressionValue: Works well as a flexible early-party anchor while you explore, catch, and test different team members.
starters.teamPlan: Pair Bubble with Grass and Fire coverage so your early team can handle more wild encounters and dungeon situations.
starters.bestFor: Players who want a steady starter choice with easy first-team flexibility.
starters.avoid: Do not treat a safe starter as a full team; Evomon rewards broader collection and coverage.

starters.name: Early Team Formula
starters.progressionValue: A practical first team should include your starter, two different-element catches, and one backup or utility pick.
starters.teamPlan: Use Coins and EXP Fruits to support the Evomons you actually battle with, while saving better catching items for rare or stronger encounters.
starters.bestFor: New players who want steady progress through catching, training, exploration, and early PVE.
starters.avoid: Do not chase Shiny or Sparkle forms before your battle team can reliably clear early fights.

evomonTierList.title: Evomon Tier List and Team Building Guide
evomonTierList.subtitle: Build around battle role, matchup coverage, evolution value, PvE strength, dungeon utility, and PvP pressure.
evomonTierList.intro: Evomon is a Roblox monster-catching adventure focused on catching, training, evolving, and turn-based battles with a roster of 200+ Evomons. This tier grid ranks practical team roles so players can adapt to their own roster, levels, skills, and rare-form goals.

cards.name: Evolved Core Carry
cards.use: Invest regular EXP and EXP Fruits here first, then build the rest of the team around the carry's weak matchups.

cards.name: Dungeon-Ready Anchor
cards.role: Reliable partner for longer co-op fights
cards.use: Keep at least one trained Evomon that can stay useful when battles last longer than a quick wild encounter.

cards.name: Coverage Specialist
cards.use: Add coverage before chasing duplicate favorites, because a balanced team clears more content than repeated versions of the same role.

cards.name: PvP Pressure Pick
cards.use: Use this slot to test how your skills perform against real opponents instead of judging only by wild encounters.

cards.name: Fresh Evolution Project
cards.use: Keep it in the upgrade queue, but do not drain every reward into it before it proves a clear job on the team.

cards.name: Rare Variant Collector
cards.use: Train rare variants after your main battle team can already handle normal progression.

cards.name: Duplicate Catch
cards.use: Store or rotate later; do not replace a trained core pick just because a new catch looks exciting.

cards.name: Underleveled Specialty Pick
cards.use: Save it for targeted testing once your main team has stable levels and coverage.

evomonCatchingGuide.title: Evomon Shiny, Sparkle, Eggs, and Catching Guide
evomonCatchingGuide.subtitle: Use catching, egg hatching, evolution, and rare-form hunting to grow a stronger collection without wasting early progress.
evomonCatchingGuide.intro: Evomon's collection loop centers on catching wild Evomons, training them, evolving them, hatching eggs, and chasing rare Shiny and Sparkle forms across a 200+ Evomon roster.

items.title: Catching Comes Before Perfecting
items.summary: Catch enough Evomons to build a functional team before spending heavily on rare-form hunting.
items.content: Start by catching Evomons that add new battle roles or matchup coverage. A wider roster gives you more options for PvE fights, dungeons, bosses, and PvP testing.
items.action: Keep a main battle team and a separate collection bench so every new catch does not interrupt your strongest lineup.

items.title: Eggs Feed Long-Term Collection Growth
items.summary: Egg hatching is part of the official Evomon loop and connects naturally with evolution progress.
items.content: Treat eggs as new roster sources: hatch them, check whether the result fills a missing role, then decide whether it deserves training. Evolution matters most when the Evomon improves your team instead of just adding another unused form.
items.action: Hatch first, compare roles second, invest third.

items.title: Shiny and Sparkle Forms Are Collection Goals
items.summary: Shiny and Sparkle Evomons are the rare-form chase for collectors and showcase teams.
items.content: Rare forms are worth chasing, but early players should build a working team first. After the main team can handle normal progression, hunt rare variants with a clear route and enough resources.
items.action: Prioritize rare variants that can also become useful in your trained team.

items.title: Prioritize New Roles Over Duplicates
items.summary: A new matchup role usually helps more than another copy of a role you already trained.
items.content: When choosing what to catch next, look for Evomons that expand your battle options. A collection that covers more situations performs better in PvE, dungeons, and PvP than a pile of repeated roles.
items.action: Use duplicates for collection completion and future testing, not immediate core investment.

items.title: Turn Rare Hunting Into a Routine
items.summary: Shiny, Sparkle, egg, and evolution goals work best when they are handled in repeatable sessions.
items.content: Rotate between catching new Evomons, hatching eggs, training useful finds, and checking whether rare variants are worth moving into your active team. This keeps collection progress steady without freezing battle progression.
items.action: Run collection sessions after your main team can clear normal battles comfortably.

evomonLevelingGuide.title: Evomon Leveling, Evolution, and EXP Fruit Guide
evomonLevelingGuide.subtitle: Level your strongest Evomons first, evolve with a team purpose, and use EXP Fruits where they create the biggest progression gain.
evomonLevelingGuide.intro: Evomon progression is built around catching, training, evolving, and battling. EXP Fruits support faster leveling, but the strongest route is to feed progress into a core team instead of scattering upgrades across every new catch.

steps.title: Finish the Basic Catch-and-Battle Loop
steps.action: Start by learning how wild encounters, catching, training, and turn-based battles work.
steps.why: A player who understands the normal loop can judge which Evomons deserve investment instead of upgrading blindly.
steps.avoid: Do not spend progression rewards before you know which Evomons actually help you win fights.

steps.title: Choose One Core Carry
steps.action: Pick one battle-ready Evomon as your main damage source for PvE progression and boss preparation.
steps.why: A focused carry clears fights faster and gives the rest of the team a stable point to build around.
steps.avoid: Do not level every new catch evenly in the early game; that creates a wide roster with no reliable winner.

steps.title: Use EXP Fruits on Core Team Members
steps.action: Spend Medium EXP Fruits on your carry and the coverage Evomons that solve its weak matchups.
steps.why: EXP Fruits give faster leveling value when used on Evomons that stay in your active team.
steps.avoid: Do not use EXP Fruits on duplicates or untested rare-form catches before your main team is stable.

steps.title: Farm Battles With a Purpose
steps.action: Use repeated battles to raise levels, test skills, and confirm which Evomons are ready for bosses, dungeons, and PvP.
steps.why: Training is more valuable when each fight tests a specific role: carry damage, coverage, survival, or skill timing.
steps.avoid: Do not grind random fights without checking whether your team is actually improving.

steps.title: Evolve for Team Value
steps.action: Move evolution priority toward Evomons that gain a clearer job after training and fit your active lineup.
steps.why: Evolution value is strongest when it improves progression, dungeon readiness, or PvP pressure.
steps.avoid: Do not evolve only for novelty while ignoring team coverage and battle performance.

steps.title: Test the Team in Harder Content
steps.action: Use map bosses, co-op dungeon runs, and PvP battles to check whether your leveled team is ready for the next step.
steps.why: Harder content exposes weak coverage, underleveled picks, and skills that only looked good in simple fights.
steps.avoid: Do not judge a full team only from easy wild encounters.

evomonHardContent.title: Evomon Dungeons, Boss Fights, PvP, and Mounts Guide
evomonHardContent.subtitle: Move from casual catching into map bosses, co-op dungeons, PvP battles, powerful skills, and legendary mounts.
evomonHardContent.intro: Evomon starts with exploration and catching, then expands into harder content built around strategy, skills, multiplayer dungeons, boss fights, PvP, and rideable legendary Evomons. Use this ladder to decide when your team is ready to push into harder goals.

items.when: Immediately after entering the game world.
items.title: Exploration Foundation
items.objective: Catch wild Evomons, learn the map flow, and build a roster before pushing harder fights.
tips: keep collection and battle teams separate
items.payoff: A wider roster gives you more choices for bosses, dungeons, and PvP.

items.when: After your main team can clear normal battles consistently.
items.title: Map Boss Fights
items.objective: Use bosses as team checks for levels, skills, matchup coverage, and survival.
items.payoff: Bosses reveal whether your team is ready for harder PvE.

items.when: After you have at least one reliable dungeon-ready Evomon.
items.title: Co-op Dungeons
items.objective: Team up with friends and clear longer fights that reward coordination and consistent battle roles.
tips: avoid bringing only underleveled collection picks
save fragile experiments for easier content
items.payoff: Dungeons turn individual collection progress into team-based progression.

items.when: After your team has a carry, coverage, and at least one skill-focused pressure pick.
items.title: PvP Battles
items.objective: Test turn-based strategy against real opponents instead of only scripted encounters.
items.payoff: PvP shows which Evomons perform under pressure and which only looked strong in easy fights.

items.when: As soon as your Evomons gain meaningful battle options.
items.title: Powerful Skills
items.objective: Use skills to control battle tempo, punish bad matchups, and improve boss or PvP performance.
tips: match skills to each Evomon's role
do not judge by visuals alone
items.payoff: A well-trained skill plan makes the same roster perform better without needing constant replacements.

items.when: After basic exploration is comfortable and you are ready to chase prestige goals.
items.title: Legendary Mounts
items.objective: Ride legendary Evomons across the world and turn collection progress into visible account progress.
tips: keep progressing combat while chasing them
use exploration routes to support collection goals
items.payoff: Mounts improve the feel of exploration and give high-value collection targets.

items.when: After casual catching no longer blocks your main team.
items.title: Endgame Progression Ladder
items.objective: Cycle between collection, leveling, evolution, bosses, dungeons, PvP, and mount goals.
tips: hunt rare forms in planned sessions
use hard content to expose team gaps
items.payoff: A clear ladder keeps progression moving and turns collection, combat, dungeons, PvP, and mounts into one connected route.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Evomon

questions.question: What is Evomon?
questions.answer: Evomon is a Roblox open-world monster-catching adventure where players collect 200+ Evomons, hunt Shiny and Sparkle variants, train teams, battle turn by turn, hatch eggs, ride legendary creatures, and clear dungeons with friends.

questions.question: Where can I play Evomon?
questions.answer: Play Evomon from the official Roblox experience page published by Evomon Devs. Always start from the official page instead of mirror sites.

questions.question: How do I redeem Evomon codes?
questions.answer: Open the cogwheel Settings menu in Evomon, paste an active code into the code input box, and press OK to claim your reward. Finish the opening tutorial first if the Settings menu is not available.

questions.question: Which Evomon starter should I pick?
questions.answer: Evomon offers three starters: Leafbun (Grass), Blazpup (Fire), and Bubble (Water). Pick based on the first role you want, then catch other types early to cover weaknesses.

questions.question: What are Shiny and Sparkle Evomons?
questions.answer: Shiny and Sparkle Evomons are rare variants and long-term collection goals. Hunt them after your main battle team can handle normal progression.

questions.question: Does Evomon support multiplayer?
questions.answer: Yes. Evomon includes multiplayer features like teaming up with friends and taking on co-op dungeons as part of the wider PVE loop.
